Tests strict mode error when assigning a value to the `arguments` object.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that assigns the value `1` to the global `arguments` object. The `arguments` object is typically used within functions to access the arguments passed to that function. By assigning a value to `arguments` at the global scope, this script overrides the default behavior of the `arguments` object, which can lead to unexpected behavior in functions that rely on it. The script uses strict mode, indicated by `"use strict"`, which enforces stricter parsing and error handling in JavaScript, but in this case, it does not prevent the reassignment of `arguments` at the global level.
